notes: 5772b37e (2026-07-10T15:32:47Z) CI hygiene commit #772 — fail notebook-tests job when a changed notebook fails to execute. Response to issue #375 (metaprompt notebook that failed first API call for every user, shipped to production undetected). Changes: (a) execution step always writes exec_failures count as output (previously only when non-zero); (b) Final status check fails the job when exec_failures > 0; (c) Final status check fails when execution step itself errors under continue-on-error. Severity 🟡 (Cat 6 default ⚠ possible; borderline ⚠/🟡 → down per doctrine because CI-hygiene commit for notebook-test infrastructure is not kit-integration-load-bearing on its own; underlying #375 metaprompt-notebook-first-API-call-failure-shipped-to-production-undetected pattern is worth naming as CI-hygiene-vs-notebook-example-quality primitive in KB-02 §template-quality-gates if surfaces in Cat 4 practitioner blog or extends across Cat 6 cookbook commits within 14 days). Reset consecutive_empty_scans from 3 → 0.
